records.title: Advanced Rectifier Technologies for Electrolysis-Based Hydrogen Production: A Comparative Study and Real-World Applications
records.authors_or_org: Gao, Y.; Wang, X.; Meng, X.
records.claim_supported: Energies (MDPI, vol. 18, issue 1, online 2024-12-27 / issue-dated 2025) primary comparative study of rectifier technologies (thyristor, IGBT/transistor, hybrid) for electrolysis-based hydrogen production with real-world application benchmarks.
records.notes: Companion piece to L11-016 on the rectifier/power-electronics side of electrolyzer precision power delivery. | Companion piece to L11-016. Full author list now confirmed via Crossref.

records.title: Power quality improvement for 20 MW PEM water electrolysis system
records.authors_or_org: Keddar, M.; Zhang, Z.; Periasamy, C.; Doumbia, M. L.
records.claim_supported: International Journal of Hydrogen Energy (vol. 47, issue 95, Dec 2022) primary engineering case study on power-quality improvement for a 20 MW-scale PEM water electrolysis system.
records.notes: DOI and full author list now confirmed via Crossref.

records.title: Power quality and reactive power of water electrolyzers supplied with thyristor converters
records.authors_or_org: Ruuskanen, V.; Koponen, J.; Kosonen, A.; Niemelä, M.; Ahola, J.; Hämäläinen, A.
records.claim_supported: Journal of Power Sources (vol. 459, May 2020) primary research: 6-pulse thyristor rectifiers for MW-scale electrolyzer stacks have poor power factor at partial load and dominant distortion-reactive-power components; conventional thyristor structures with high current ripple consume up to 13% more energy than optimized process current sources, and transistor-based converters can cut specific energy consumption by up to 14% vs. 6-pulse thyristor rectifiers.
records.notes: DOI now confirmed via Crossref (raw record had no DOI). Directly load-bearing for L11's precision-power-delivery pain point.

records.title: Power quality estimation of water electrolyzers based on current and voltage measurements
records.authors_or_org: Ruuskanen, V.; Koponen, J.; Kosonen, A.; Hehemann, M.; Keller, R.; Niemelä, M.; Ahola, J.
records.claim_supported: Journal of Power Sources (vol. 450, Feb 2020) primary research method for estimating electrolyzer power-quality metrics directly from current/voltage measurements; establishes that current harmonics generate additional losses beyond the mean-value hydrogen production rate.
records.notes: DOI now confirmed via Crossref. Companion methodology paper to L11-019, same LUT/Finnish research cluster.

records.title: Impact of Power Converter Current Ripple on the Degradation of PEM Electrolyzer Performances
records.authors_or_org: Parache, F.; Schneider, H.; Turpin, C.; Richet, N.; Debellemanière, O.; Bru, E.; Thieu, A. T.; Bertail, C.; Marot, C.
records.claim_supported: Membranes (MDPI, vol. 12, issue 2, 2022-01-19) primary research: ripple current from rectifier supply increases internal equivalent resistance in PEM electrolyzer cells, accelerating degradation; average power consumption rises with ripple factor and falls with ripple frequency, while hydrogen flow rate (set by mean current) is unaffected.
records.notes: Journal/DOI/authors now fully confirmed via PMC citation metadata and Crossref (raw record had DOI unresolved).

records.title: Redox-mediated decoupled seawater direct splitting for H2 production
records.authors_or_org: Liu, T.; Lan, C.; Tang, M.; Li, M.; Xu, Y.; Yang, H.; Deng, Q.; Jiang, W.; Zhao, Z.; Wu, Y.; Xie, H.
records.claim_supported: Nature Communications (vol. 15, 2024-10-15) primary research introducing a redox-mediated (ferricyanide/ferrocyanide electron-shuttle) strategy to suppress the competing chlorine-evolution reaction, enabling a decoupled seawater direct-electrolysis system with a separate O2-evolution reactor, stable in chloride-saturated environments at low voltage.
records.notes: Relevant to precision electrochemical process control for direct seawater feedstock, an emerging alternative to deionized-water electrolysis. | Full author list now confirmed via Crossref.

records.title: In-situ direct seawater electrolysis using floating platform in ocean with uncontrollable wave motion
records.authors_or_org: Liu, T.; Zhao, Z.; Tang, W.; Chen, Y.; Lan, C.; Zhu, L.; Jiang, W.; Wu, Y.; Wang, Y.; Yang, Z.; Yang, D.; Wang, Q.; Luo, L.; Liu, T.; Xie, H.
records.claim_supported: Nature Communications (vol. 15, 2024-06-21) primary research demonstrating, for the first time per the authors, direct seawater electrolysis on a floating ocean platform under uncontrolled wave motion.
records.notes: First-of-kind field demonstration relevant to offshore/marine hydrogen production power-control engineering. | Full author list now confirmed via Crossref. Same research group as L11-022 (Xie, H. et al.).

records.title: Direct seawater electrolysis for hydrogen production
records.authors_or_org: Yu, L.; Ning, M.; Wang, Y.; Yuan, C.; Ren, Z.
records.claim_supported: Nature Reviews Materials (vol. 10, issue 11, pp. 857-873, 2025-07-23) review of direct seawater electrolysis covering chloride-induced corrosion, parasitic chlorine evolution, membrane degradation, and catalyst instability as core unresolved challenges.
records.notes: Review-tier T2; useful as the state-of-field synthesis for the seawater-electrolysis sub-cluster (L11-022, L11-023). | Review-tier T2; state-of-field synthesis for the seawater-electrolysis sub-cluster (L11-022, L11-023). Full author list now confirmed via Crossref.

records.title: Techno-economic Study of an Electrolysis-Based Green Ammonia Production Plant
records.authors_or_org: Sousa, J.; Waiblinger, W.; Friedrich, K. A.
records.claim_supported: Industrial & Engineering Chemistry Research (vol. 61, issue 39, 2022-09-21) primary techno-economic analysis of an electrolysis-based (Haber-Bosch-coupled) green ammonia production plant, quantifying capex/opex drivers.
records.notes: Bottom-up cost-modeling source useful for future TAM/unit-economics triangulation on green ammonia. | Full author list now confirmed via Crossref.

records.title: High Current Density Copper Electrorefining and Electrowinning in a Series Cell Part II: Electrowinning
records.authors_or_org: Harvey, W. W.; Randlett, M. R.; Bangerskis, K. I.
records.claim_supported: JOM (Springer, on behalf of TMS, vol. 27, issue 8, Aug 1975) primary research on high-current-density copper electrowinning performance in a series-cell configuration.
records.notes: Publication year not confirmed this session (BF-prefix DOI indicates a pre-2000s Springer conversion); verifier must confirm exact year/volume before acceptance. | Year corrected from null to 1975 (confirmed via Crossref); authors now confirmed.

records.title: Novel Additives in Copper Electrorefining -- Small Laboratory Scale
records.authors_or_org: Kowalik, P.; Kopyto, D.; Leszczyńska-Sejda, K.; Simka, W.
records.claim_supported: Materials (MDPI, vol. 17, issue 6, article 1262, 2024-03-08) primary lab-scale study of novel electrolyte additive combinations for copper electrorefining, reporting current efficiency of ~97% (ionic-liquid/thiourea additive combination) and smooth cathode deposits.
records.notes: Journal/DOI/authors now fully confirmed (raw record had all unresolved).

records.title: Mechanical Testing Methods for Assessing Hydrogen Embrittlement in Pipeline Steels: A Review
records.authors_or_org: Paterlini, L.; Re, G.; Curia, A.; Ormellese, M.; Bolzoni, F.
records.claim_supported: Metals (MDPI, vol. 15, issue 10, 2025-10-10) review of mechanical testing protocols for hydrogen-embrittlement assessment of pipeline steels, emphasizing design-oriented tensile/fracture-mechanics approaches and the influence of hydrogen partial pressure and deformation rate on cross-study comparability.
records.notes: Explicitly framed as review -- T2. | Explicitly framed as review -- T2. Full author list now confirmed via Crossref.

records.title: Fracture Toughness Assessment of Pipeline Steels Under Hydrogen Exposure for Blended Gas Applications
records.authors_or_org: Ghadiani, H.; Farhat, Z.; Alam, T.; Islam, Md. A.
records.claim_supported: Metals (MDPI, vol. 15, issue 1, 2025-01-01) primary experimental research assessing fracture toughness of pipeline steels under hydrogen exposure for hydrogen/natural-gas blending applications.
records.notes: Primary experimental study, not a review, distinguishing it from L11-028 and L11-030. | Primary experimental study, not a review, distinguishing it from L11-028 and L11-030. Full author list now confirmed via Crossref.

records.title: Addressing the Hydrogen Embrittlement Challenge in Future Hydrogen Pipelines: A Multiscale Review from Mechanisms to Material Design
records.authors_or_org: Zheng, Z.; Liu, D.; Sun, X.; Wang, Y.; Zhao, Y.; Xu, J.
records.claim_supported: Metals (MDPI, vol. 16, issue 4, 2026-04-17) multiscale review of hydrogen-embrittlement mechanisms and material-design mitigation strategies for future hydrogen pipeline networks.
records.notes: Review-tier T2. | Review-tier T2. Full author list now confirmed via Crossref.

records.title: Analysis of PEM Water Electrolyzer Failure Due to Induced Hydrogen Crossover in Catalyst-Coated PFSA Membranes
records.authors_or_org: Kuhnert, E.; Heidinger, M.; Sandu, D.; Hacker, V.; Bodner, M.
records.claim_supported: Membranes (MDPI, vol. 13, issue 3, article 348, 2023-03-17) primary failure-analysis research on PEM water electrolyzer failure caused by induced hydrogen crossover through catalyst-coated PFSA membranes.
records.notes: Journal/DOI/authors now fully confirmed (raw record had all unresolved; had replaced an arXiv discovery-only placeholder per scout notes).

records.title: Regional Clean Hydrogen Hubs award announcement -- up to $2.2 billion for Gulf Coast (HyVelocity) and Midwest (MachH2) Hubs
records.authors_or_org: U.S. Department of Energy
records.claim_supported: DOE announced (2024-11-20) up to $2.2B combined federal cost share for two Regional Clean Hydrogen Hubs: Gulf Coast Hydrogen Hub (HyVelocity, TX, up to $1.2B, ~45,000 direct jobs, electrolysis + natural gas w/ CCS) and Midwest Hydrogen Hub (MachH2, IL/IN/IA/MI, up to $1B, ~12,000 direct jobs, targeting steel/glass/manufacturing/power/refining/heavy transport decarbonization).
records.notes: One of seven total H2Hubs awarded under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law's up-to-$7B program; this record covers the two Nov 2024 Phase awards specifically. | One of seven total H2Hubs awarded under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law's up-to-$7B program.

records.title: Manufacturing Cost Analysis for PEM Electrolyzers and Perspectives for Future Cost Reduction
records.authors_or_org: Brauch, J.; Skangos, C.; Sittler, L.; Badgett, A. (National Renewable Energy Laboratory / H2NEW, 2025 Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Seminar)
records.claim_supported: NREL/H2NEW conference presentation (OSTI record, Report No. NREL/PR-6A20-92558) on manufacturing cost modeling for 1 MW PEM electrolyzer systems: stack costs are dominated by iridium/platinum catalyst material costs; power electronics and hydrogen purification equipment are the largest balance-of-plant cost contributors; manufacturing scale alone provides limited cost-reduction potential, requiring combined strategies (materials, design, scale) to achieve significant electrolyzer cost reduction.
records.notes: Original docs.nrel.gov PDF and research-hub.nrel.gov / www.nrel.gov all failed DNS resolution across multiple attempts this session (nrel.gov domain appears blocked in this sandboxed environment); replaced with the equivalent OSTI.GOV conference record for the same report (matching NREL report number PR-6A20-92558) per verifier substitution allowance. The raw scout's specific $/kW capital-cost figures ($2,000/kW, $1,500-2,500/kW range) were NOT present in the fetched OSTI abstract and are dropped pending independent confirmation -- not invented.

records.title: Optimal operation of solid-oxide electrolysis cells considering long-term chemical degradation
records.authors_or_org: Giridhar, N. V.; Allan, D. A.; Li, M.; Zitney, S. E.; Biegler, L. T.; Bhattacharyya, D.
records.claim_supported: Energy Conversion and Management (Elsevier, vol. 319, Nov 2024) primary research: dynamic optimization of SOEC operation accounting for long-term chemical degradation cuts levelized cost of hydrogen by 9.5% vs. degradation-focused strategies and 16% vs. efficiency-focused strategies; optimal cell-replacement schedules range 2-5 years depending on electricity pricing and operating mode.
records.notes: RECLASSIFIED from source_type 'national_lab'/peer_review_status 'not_applicable' (raw scout classification) to 'academic_peer_reviewed'/'verified' this session: the OSTI record is a repository mirror of a genuine Elsevier journal article (DOI confirmed via Crossref), not merely a lab report. Authors are NETL/West Virginia University/Carnegie Mellon University researchers.

records.title: Global Hydrogen Review 2025
records.authors_or_org: International Energy Agency (IEA)
records.claim_supported: IEA Global Hydrogen Review 2025: global electrolyzer installed capacity reached 2 GW in 2024 (+1 GW more through July 2025); China holds 65% of global installed/FID capacity and ~60% of global electrolyzer manufacturing capacity (20 GW/yr nameplate vs. ~2 GW 2024 demand); confirmed low-emissions H2 production pipeline for 2030 fell to 37 Mtpa potential (from 49 Mtpa in 2024), with only 4.2 Mtpa from projects with FID/operational status; a 240 MW electrolyzer in Vietnam is one of few non-China projects at that scale to reach FID.
records.notes: Central annual benchmark report for the whole lane; load-bearing for market-size and China-dominance claims.

records.title: 低炭素水素等サプライチェーンの構築に向けた「価格差に着目した支援」及び「拠点整備支援」の実施について
records.authors_or_org: JOGMEC (Japan Organization for Metals and Energy Security, formerly Japan Oil, Gas and Metals National Corporation) / METI
records.claim_supported: JOGMEC press release (fetched): describes two support schemes under Japan's Hydrogen Society Promotion Act (effective 2024-10-23) -- 'price-differential support' subsidizing the gap between domestic/imported low-carbon-hydrogen costs and existing fuel prices, and 'base/hub-development support' subsidizing FEED and infrastructure costs for jointly-developed hydrogen transport/storage facilities, administered by JOGMEC on METI's behalf.
records.notes: Japanese-language primary government source (local-language primary #1). Confirms the JOGMEC/METI CfD administrative mechanism referenced across multiple English secondary reports. | Japanese-language primary government source (local-language primary #1). The aggregate JPY 3 trillion (~$19-20B) figure cited in secondary English-language reporting was not stated on this specific page and should be attributed to that separate coverage, not this canonical record directly. JOGMEC's English organization name updated to reflect its 2024 rename.

records.title: 2025년 청정수소발전시장 경쟁입찰 공고
records.authors_or_org: Korea Power Exchange (KPX)
records.claim_supported: Korea Power Exchange (KPX) Notice 2025-02 (issued 2025-05-09), an official competitive-bidding announcement for the 2025 clean hydrogen power generation market, issued under Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy Notice No. 2024-125 governing annual hydrogen-power purchase-volume calculation; bidding conducted via the Low-Carbon Central Contract Integration Platform (kchps.kmos.kr).
records.notes: Korean-language primary government tender source (local-language primary #2). | Korean-language primary government tender source (local-language primary #2). Specific procurement-volume figures were not visible in the fetched bulletin summary (require a linked attachment); the raw scout's cited 0.22/3.9/27.9 Mt/yr national hydrogen-supply trajectory figures come from separate government planning documents, not this specific tender notice, and are dropped from the confirmed claim.

records.title: Six winners of the 2024 Innovation Fund hydrogen auction sign grant agreements (European Hydrogen Bank, IF24 auction)
records.authors_or_org: European Climate, Infrastructure and Environment Executive Agency (CINEA) / European Commission
records.claim_supported: CINEA/European Commission press release (fetched, dated 2026-01-20): the European Hydrogen Bank's first auction (IF24, closed Feb 2024, 132 bids) awarded ~EUR720M to 7 renewable-hydrogen projects; six of those seven signed grant agreements by Jan 2026 totaling EUR270.6M (individual grants EUR1.8M-135.5M), spanning Spain/Finland/Norway with combined 381.25 MW electrolyser capacity and ~500 kt renewable H2 output over a decade (~3.4 Mt CO2 avoided); two projects received >EUR35M each under a dedicated maritime-fuel topic; funded from EU ETS revenues; recipients must reach financial close within 2.5 years and begin production within 5 years, with fixed-premium support for up to 10 years.
records.notes: Consolidated record covering all three EHB auction rounds identified this session; EU official procurement/subsidy-auction demand evidence with real signed grant agreements, not just announced intentions. | This record narrowly covers Round 1 (IF24) grant-agreement signing only, the specific event described in the fetched URL. The raw scout's claims about a second EUR992M/15-project round (closed Feb 2025) and a third >EUR1B/9-project round (Dec2025-Feb2026) were not verified on this specific page this session and are not part of this record's confirmed claim -- flagged as needing separate sourcing if cited later, per 'conflicts shown, not averaged away.'

records.title: Elyzer (formerly Silyzer) PEM electrolyzer product line -- hydrogen solutions overview
records.authors_or_org: Siemens Energy
records.claim_supported: Siemens Energy official hydrogen-solutions page (fetched): the PEM electrolyzer product line, renamed 'Elyzer' (formerly 'Silyzer'), is offered in modular configurations up to gigawatt scale; named reference installations include Luxcara (Hamburg, 100 MW), BASF (Germany, 54 MW, ~72,000 t H2/yr), and H2 Future (Austria, 6 MW).
records.notes: Product renamed Silyzer 300 -> Elyzer P-300 per this session's research. The raw scout's specific Silyzer 300 performance figures (17.5 MW/24-stack array, 335 kg H2/hour, >75.5% plant efficiency) originate from Siemens' own datasheet PDF (assets.siemens-energy.com), corroborated only by WebSearch synthesis of that PDF, not independently machine-read as text this session -- retained as an unconfirmed vendor claim, not asserted as directly verified.

records.title: HyLYZER 1000 PEM electrolyzer system product page
records.authors_or_org: Accelera by Cummins
records.claim_supported: Accelera (Cummins) official HyLYZER 1000 product page (fetched): 1,000 Nm3/h (90 kg/h) hydrogen output at 30 barg without a compressor; 5 MW rated power with 15-100% dynamic operating range; GW-scale modular manufacturing referenced.
records.notes: The raw scout's broader claims (1.25 MW-200+ MW product-line span across HyLYZER-500/1000/5000, ISO 22734 compliance, ATEX rating, >500,000 cumulative operating hours) were not present on this specific product page and are dropped from the confirmed claim, pending future re-verification against the unreadable PDF or another source. Cummins/Accelera manufactures in Fridley, Minnesota per prior (not re-confirmed this session) press coverage.

records.title: John Cockerill eyes 18GW/yr electrolyser output by 2030
records.authors_or_org: Argus Media
records.claim_supported: Argus Media article (fetched): John Cockerill targets expansion from <1 GW/yr current electrolyser manufacturing capacity to 8 GW/yr by 2025 and >18 GW/yr by 2030, holding a reported 33% global electrolyser-sales market share (2021); planned capacity split includes France (new gigafactory operational end-2023), China (600 MW/yr -> 2 GW/yr), and India (+2 GW/yr), with further US/Middle East/North Africa sites planned; the company has supplied major Chinese hydrogen projects including 153 MW delivered in 2021 to Ningxia Baofeng Energy and Sinopec's Kuqa project.
records.notes: Cross-listed geography CN/IN because of the named manufacturing-capacity split; useful incumbent-manufacturing-footprint evidence. | The raw scout's claim of a July 2025 McPhy-asset acquisition was NOT found in this fetched article and is dropped from the confirmed claim pending separate verification -- a correction, not an invention.

records.title: Battery machinery: from gigascale down to precision manufacturing
records.authors_or_org: Best Magazine (trade press)
records.claim_supported: Best Magazine battery-machinery trade article (fetched): names Hioki (Japan) -- RM3546 nanohm-class resistance meter for weld-quality inspection (1 nOhm resolution); Andritz Schuler -- commissioned a 1.5 GW mass battery-formation line in Germany; Wuxi Lead (LEAD) -- dry-coating system at +/-2 micron precision, up to 100 m/min, 35% energy-consumption reduction vs. wet coating; Yinghe Technology -- cell-stacking at ~0.1 s/piece; Hitachi High-Tech -- expanded X-ray contaminant-inspection systems; Chroma (Taiwan) named among formation/test equipment suppliers.
records.notes: Trade-press level source on precision test/formation equipment incumbents relevant to L11's battery-formation-equipment scope. | Chroma ATE's specific product portfolio was named but not detailed with quantitative specs in this fetch; the raw scout's broader claim about deployment 'at major Asian and international battery makers' is a reasonable general characterization but not independently itemized here.

records.title: UHPC (Ultra-High Precision Coulometry) battery cycler product page
records.authors_or_org: Avrion Labs (redirect target of NOVONIX cell-testing-equipment page)
records.claim_supported: Avrion Labs UHPC product page (fetched): current ranges from 100 uA to 20 A with measurement precision down to 2 nA; supports formation, DCIR, HPPC, calendar/cycle-aging, and rate testing; positioned for early degradation detection and materials R&D beyond conventional cycler resolution.
records.notes: The novonixgroup.com URL now redirects to avrionlabs.com, indicating a corporate rebrand or divestiture of NOVONIX's cell-testing-equipment business between the raw scout's session and this verification pass -- flagged for idea-generation/red-team awareness since it affects incumbent-mapping accuracy for L11's battery-formation-equipment sub-scope.

records.title: Copper Electrowinning: Advanced Rectifier Systems and Power Supplies
records.authors_or_org: Advint
records.claim_supported: Advint (vendor) article on copper-electrowinning rectifier technology (fetched): contrasts SCR-thyristor rectifiers (high power handling, proven reliability, but lower efficiency/higher harmonic distortion/slower response) against IGBT switch-mode rectifiers (up to ~95% energy efficiency, better power factor, lower harmonic distortion, faster/more precise current control), positioning IGBT as the efficiency-forward option. Claims about recovery-rate and cost benefits are general/qualitative rather than quantified.
records.notes: Vendor/consultancy content -- context only, not independent validation. Directly names the precision-DC-power-delivery equipment category (rectifiers) central to L11's electrowinning scope. | Vendor content -- context only, not independent validation, per source standards. The raw scout's specific 200-375 A/m^2 current-density figure was not located in this fetch and is dropped from the confirmed claim.

records.title: Plug Power Reports Q4 and Full Year 2025 Results with Strong Sales Growth and Margin Expansion
records.authors_or_org: Plug Power Inc.
records.claim_supported: Plug Power reported a record $187 million in electrolyzer revenue for 2025 against an approximately $8 billion global sales funnel; named 2025 deployments include 100 MW (ten 10-MW GenEco PEM arrays) installed at Galp's Sines refinery (Portugal, ~15,000 t/yr target), a 55 MW award from Carlton Power across three UK green-hydrogen projects, and five 5-MW containerized units delivered to Iberdrola's Castellon site (Spain); cumulative shipments exceed 300 MW of GenEco electrolyzers across six continents.
records.notes: Company self-reported filing/press release -- treat revenue and backlog figures as company claims pending cross-check against the 10-K when the verifier pass runs. | Company self-reported filing/press release -- treat revenue and backlog figures as company claims pending cross-check against the 10-K.

records.title: Nel ASA: Fourth quarter 2025 financial results
records.authors_or_org: Nel ASA
records.claim_supported: Nel ASA Q4/FY2025 results press release (fetched directly, HTML): total order backlog NOK 1,319M at FY2025-end (down 18% y/y vs. Q4 2024, though up 34% vs. Q3 2025) -- PEM segment backlog NOK 878M, Alkaline segment NOK 440M; Q4 2025 order intake NOK 686M (+364% y/y vs. Q4 2024's NOK 148M), including a 40 MW/>USD50M contract from HYDS, the PEM division's largest single purchase order to date; Q4 revenue from contracts with customers NOK 330M (-20% y/y), total revenue and income NOK 361M (vs. NOK 450M Q4 2024); CEO commentary attributes the pattern to FIDs 'taking longer' and shifting project milestones during 2025; the company reached FID for its next-generation pressurized-alkaline production line, commercial launch scheduled 2026-05-06.
records.notes: Replaces the raw scout's unreadable PDF fetch with a directly-readable HTML press release for the same reporting period, resolving the raw record's PDF-extraction failure. Confirms 18% y/y backlog decline (supporting the lane brief's 'hydrogen boom vs. shrinking order books' contradiction) while also showing a 34% quarter-over-quarter uptick -- both true simultaneously, preserved rather than reconciled.

records.title: thyssenkrupp nucera returns to profit in 2024/2025 despite decline in sales (FY2024/25 results)
records.authors_or_org: thyssenkrupp nucera AG & Co. KGaA
records.claim_supported: thyssenkrupp nucera FY2024/25: order backlog fell to EUR 0.6B (from EUR 1.1B prior year) as of Sept 30 2025; FY sales EUR 845M (vs EUR 862M prior year); order intake fell to EUR 348M (from EUR 636M). Green-hydrogen (gH2) segment: sales EUR 459M, EBIT -EUR 56M, order intake only EUR 26M. Chlor-Alkali (CA) segment: sales EUR 386M (+double-digit growth), EBIT +EUR 58M, order intake EUR 322M. Named contracts: AGC Vinythai chlor-alkali plant commissioned; PGS selected tk nucera as preferred supplier for a 1.4 GW electrolyzer project in Australia; TGV SRAAC selected for an expansion contract in India.
records.notes: Strong contradictory demand signal: chlor-alkali (mature, non-hydrogen electrochemical manufacturing) is profitable and growing while green-hydrogen electrolyzer order intake has collapsed ~96% y/y for this major OEM -- important for the lane brief's 'unresolved contradictions' section. | Strong contradictory demand signal: chlor-alkali is profitable and growing while green-hydrogen electrolyzer order intake collapsed ~96% y/y for this major OEM.

records.title: Hydrogen case study: Sinopec Kuqa project
records.authors_or_org: Wood Mackenzie (Tiantian Zhao, hydrogen consultant)
records.claim_supported: Wood Mackenzie case-study opinion piece (fetched): Sinopec's Kuqa green-hydrogen project produced 2,010 t (22.36M Nm3) of green hydrogen in December 2023 alone while operating at only ~20% of rated electrolyzer capacity during 2023; the project is expected to reach ~51% capacity utilization once it hits its targeted 20,000 t/yr run-rate; underperformance is attributed to insufficient power supply and electrolyzer inefficiency at reduced operational loads; Chinese electrolyzer manufacturers are reportedly responding with equipment improvements.
records.notes: World's largest operating green-hydrogen demonstration project as of this session; the persistent utilization gap vs. nameplate capacity is a key real-world operability/reliability data point (not just an announced project). | The raw scout's additional figures (14,900 t cumulative delivery over 800+ operating days by September 2025; the specific 260 MW/13-electrolyzer/LONGi-Peric-Cockerill-Jingli supplier breakdown) were NOT present in this specific fetched article and are dropped from the confirmed claim, likely originating from a separate PRNewswire/Sinopec release not fetched this session.

records.title: 隆基氢能、派瑞氢能等海外签约合同摘要 (LONGi Hydrogen, Peric Hydrogen and others: overseas contract signings)
records.authors_or_org: Sina Finance (新浪财经)
records.claim_supported: Sina Finance Chinese-language article (fetched): early-2025 overseas/domestic electrolyzer contract signings include (1) LONGi Hydrogen-Messer Group cooperation to supply a 1,000 Nm3/h electrolyzed-water hydrogen unit; (2) LONGi's much larger EUR7.6 billion green-hydrogen/green-methanol strategic agreement with Nigeria's federal government and APPL Hydrogen for a 1.2 Mt/yr green-methanol-derivatives project at Ibeno; (3) CSSC (Handan) Peric Hydrogen's first Indonesia contract, a hydrogen-production-and-refueling integrated station (up to 600 kg/day) for a large mine site; (4) Sungrow Hydrogen supply of multiple 1,000 Nm3/h alkaline-electrolyzer units to Oman; (5) Guofu Hydrogen delivery of a 1 MW alkaline water-electrolysis hydrogen-production skid to India.
records.notes: Chinese-language primary-market trade reporting (local-language primary #1 of 2 Chinese sources) on named electrolyzer procurement/export contracts. | The raw scout's claim that 'LONGi Hydrogen ranked first...to win Sinopec's Uxin Banner tender, supplying 24 sets of 1,000 Nm3/h alkaline electrolyzers' was NOT found in this fetched article and is dropped from the confirmed claim -- possibly conflated from a different article; flagged as unconfirmed rather than invented or silently retained. Chinese-language primary-market trade reporting (local-language primary #1 of 2).

records.title: 中能建2023年计划采购的125套电解槽，用在哪了？(Where did China Energy Construction's 2023 planned 125-set electrolyzer procurement go?)
records.authors_or_org: 碳索氢能网 (TansuoH2 / Carbon-Hydrogen Energy Network, trade press)
records.claim_supported: Chinese-language trade article (fetched, Nov 2024): China Energy Construction (中国能建/中能建) qualified 14 electrolyzer suppliers under its 2023-2024 centralized hydrogen-equipment procurement framework, but as of this article's writing actual public tenders had NOT reached the originally-planned 125-set target. The only publicly disclosed procurement to date was the Songyuan (松原) integrated green-ammonia-methanol project's 64-set tender, won by six companies: Huaguang Energy, Sungrow Hydrogen, CSSC Peric, Qingqi Shidai, Beijing Electric Power Equipment Factory, and Continental Hydrogen. Three further CEEC-affiliated projects (Zhangye, Shuangyashan, Tongyu) had not yet tendered electrolyzers as of the article date and could account for the remaining ~61 sets.
records.notes: REPLACES the raw scout's original canonical record (BJX News, m.bjx.com.cn/mnews/20250225/1428871.shtml), which could not be fetched this session (mobile URL returned encrypted/obfuscated content; desktop www.bjx.com.cn mirror 404'd; web.archive.org unavailable in this environment). This substitute source directly CONTRADICTS the raw scout's specific claim that 'LONGi Hydrogen ranked first...to win' a 125-set tender with named RMB values (7.26M/7.18M/6.276M) -- LONGi does NOT appear among the confirmed winners of the actual disclosed 64-set Songyuan tender in this fetched source. Per source-standards discipline, this conflict is preserved and flagged, not averaged away or silently corrected to match the unverifiable original claim. Chinese-language primary-market trade reporting (local-language primary #2 of 2).
